prose:
Frontier-orbital analysis for aniline

Method provenance (reproducibility details)
Property: HOMO and LUMO one-electron orbital energies and their gap (Koopmans-style), plus related reactivity indices.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density-fitting (RIJK) in PySCF v2.x.
Geometry provenance: single-point evaluation on the fixed Cartesian coordinates read from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz (no geometry relaxation; this is a DFT//input-xyz protocol).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge = 0; multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet, treated with RKS).
SCF convergence: converged in 9 cycles; maximum |ΔE| between cycles < 1 × 10⁻⁶ Hartree.
Warnings: no warnings reported by chemkit.
Live log: the calculation was streamed to the default chemkit live log file (frontier_<timestamp>.out) in the working directory while running.

Key results (all from the converged SCF):
• HOMO energy = −5.553772 eV (index 24)
• LUMO energy = −0.2489544 eV (index 25)
• HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV

Derived Koopmans-style reactivity indices:
• Vertical ionisation potential (−ε_HOMO) = 5.553772 eV
• Vertical electron affinity (−ε_LUMO) = 0.2489544 eV
• Electronegativity χ = 2.9013632 eV
• Chemical hardness η = 2.6524088 eV
• Chemical softness S = 0.37701579 (eV)⁻¹
• Electrophilicity index ω = 1.5868422 eV

These values were computed with chemkit’s frontier-orbitals skill using the settings above; provenance = computed.
